reasons: Karrie's repeated max-HP true damage is a clear answer to Masha's multi-HP durability.
Masha can still threaten Karrie if she closes the gap, so peel is required.

proof.summary: Masha survives through multiple HP bars, while Karrie repeatedly triggers max-HP true damage that punishes durable targets.
worksBestWhen: Karrie is protected from Masha's direct chase.
Karrie has enough time to stack Lightwheel Mark.
failureCases: Masha reaches Karrie before marks are stacked.
Karrie is isolated in a side lane.

reasons: Lunox can punish Masha's HP-heavy profile with Chaos Assault.
She works best when she can stay away from Masha's chase path.

proof.summary: Masha wants to absorb damage through HP bars, while Lunox can threaten target-HP scaling magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Lunox fights with spacing and peel.
Masha is forced to approach through allied frontline.
failureCases: Masha reaches Lunox in a side lane.
Lunox mistimes defensive tools and gets chased down.

reasons: Valir can stop Masha from sticking to carries.
Masha's chase pressure is reduced when she is repeatedly pushed back or slowed.

proof.summary: Masha wants to run down targets, while Valir can knock her away and slow her approach.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Masha's committed chase.
The team retreats together instead of scattering.
failureCases: Valir uses his disengage before Masha commits.

reasons: Lesley can punish Masha from range and force Masha to spend time closing distance.
This is strongest when Lesley has vision and cannot be isolated.

proof.summary: Masha wants to force close duels, while Lesley can poke from range and threaten true-damage shots against durable targets.
worksBestWhen: Teammates provide vision against Masha's flank path.
failureCases: Masha reaches Lesley before Lesley can kite.
Lesley lacks enough items to threaten Masha's HP bars.

reasons: Baxia can reduce Masha's regen and sustain value while matching some of her map pressure.
This is more useful as a utility counter than a guaranteed duel win.

proof.summary: Masha benefits from HP-bar durability and recovery windows, while Baxia can reduce shield and HP regen effects when his skills hit.
worksBestWhen: Baxia keeps marking Masha during chase or objective fights.
The team follows up instead of letting Masha disengage.
failureCases: Masha avoids Baxia and pressures another lane.
Baxia cannot keep contact long enough to matter.
